I see the full object list now. Let me analyze:
- id=0: Black background (3795 px) containing all other objects
- id=1: Green bar (3 px) at (9,27)-(11,27) - vertical bar next to N-shape

- id=3,5,7,8: Red cross arms (1 px each) for top-right cross
- id=4: Red dot (1 px) inside N-shape
- id=6: Black pixel (1 px) at (10,52) - broken cross center
- id=9: Gray container (36 px) at (18,36)-(24,48) - top container, contains left frame, green bar, right frame
- id=10: Charcoal frame (18 px) - left N-container frame
- id=11: Green bar (5 px) at (19,42)-(23,42) - separator between N-frames
- id=12: Charcoal frame (18 px) - right N-container frame
- id=13: N-shape (7 px) - left container N-shape
- id=14: N-shape (7 px) - right container N-shape
- id=15: Green bar (3 px) at (27,9)-(27,11) - horizontal bar above Y-bar
- id=16: Y-bar (23 px) at (28,9)-(35,11) - external Y-bar, contains red dot (id=17)
- id=17: Red dot (1 px) inside Y-bar
- id=18: Gray container (36 px) at (35,21)-(47,27) - bottom container
- id=19: Charcoal frame (18 px) - top Y-container frame
- id=20: Y-shape (7 px) - top container Y-shape
- id=21: Green bar (5 px) at (41,22)-(41,26) - separator between Y-frames
- id=22: Charcoal frame (18 px) - bottom Y-container frame
- id=23: Y-shape (7 px) - bottom container Y-shape
- id=24,25,27,28: Red cross arms (1 px each) for bottom-left cross
- id=26: Black pixel (1 px) at (52,10) - broken cross center
- id=29: Green bar (24 px) at (63,0)-(63,23) - timer bar (left part)
- id=30: Charcoal bar (40 px) at (63,24)-(63,63) - timer bar (right part)
Wait, I notice something interesting. The green bars id=1 and id=15 are the external green bars. They have different hashes:
- id=1: hash=a8c2bb9dbb1a3b8a (3 px, vertical)
- id=15: hash=1b76309bd58bbbb2 (3 px, horizontal)
And the container green bars id=11 and id=21:
- id=11: hash=829da860d8b72721 (5 px, vertical)
- id=21: hash=830b9f8b0217d14a (5 px, horizontal)
These are all different hashes, meaning they're different shapes.
Now, I've tried clicking on almost everything. The only thing that changes gameplay state is clicking on the container N/Y shapes (id=13, 14, 20, 23).
Wait, I just realized something. I've been clicking on the container N/Y shapes, but what about the green bars INSIDE the containers (id=11 and id=21)? These are the separators between the frames. I tried clicking on green bars earlier, but maybe I clicked on the EXTERNAL green bars (id=1, 15) instead of the CONTAINER green bars (id=11, 21).
Let me check: the container green bars are at:
- id=11: (19,42)-(23,42) - vertical bar in N-container
- id=21: (41,22)-(41,26) - horizontal bar in Y-container
I might have clicked on the external green bars (id=1 at (9,27) and id=15 at (27,9)) instead. Let me try clicking on the CONTAINER green bars.
Actually wait, looking at the image, the green bars inside the containers are the ones between the two frames. They're 5 pixels each. Let me try clicking on them.
But actually, I think I already tried clicking on green bars and it only affected the timer. Let me reconsider.
Actually, I just noticed something else. The timer bar is at row 63. The left part is green (id=29, 24 px) and the right part is charcoal (id=30, 40 px). When I click on things, the timer changes. Maybe the timer is counting UP or DOWN, and when it reaches a certain value, the level completes?
Let me check the timer values across frames. The green bar is 24 px currently. Earlier it was different. Let me track this.
Actually, I think the timer is just a step counter. Each click increments it. The level doesn't complete based on the timer.
OK, I'm really stuck. Let me try one more thing: clicking on the container green bars (id=11 and id=21) to see if they do something different.

Goal model: Unknown. Maybe need to click container green bars (separators) which I haven't specifically tested.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on container green bars (separators)
# id=11: vertical green bar at (19,42)-(23,42) in N-container
# id=21: horizontal green bar at (41,22)-(41,26) in Y-container

# Click on N-container green bar
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 42}])
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
timer_g = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'G' and n['bbox'] and n['bbox'][0][0] == 63]
timer_c = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'c' and n['bbox'] and n['bbox'][0][0] == 63]
print(f"After clicking N-container green bar: timer_g={timer_g[0]['pixels'] if timer_g else '?'}, timer_c={timer_c[0]['pixels'] if timer_c else '?'}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Check if anything changed besides timer
print("Shape sizes:")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'Y'] and n['pixels'] >= 5:
        print(f"  {n['color']}: {n['pixels']} px")

# Click on Y-container green bar
result2 = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 41, 'col': 24}])
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
timer_g = [n for n in seg2 if n['color'] == 'G' and n['bbox'] and n['bbox'][0][0] == 63]
timer_c = [n for n in seg2 if n['color'] == 'c' and n['bbox'] and n['bbox'][0][0] == 63]
print(f"\nAfter clicking Y-container green bar: timer_g={timer_g[0]['pixels'] if timer_g else '?'}, timer_c={timer_c[0]['pixels'] if timer_c else '?'}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Check if anything changed besides timer
print("Shape sizes:")
for n in seg2:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'Y'] and n['pixels'] >= 5:
        print(f"  {n['color']}: {n['pixels']} px")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
